VOL LANDSCAPE & ANALYTICS

The VIX at 27.5 sits in backwardation with VIX3M trading 190bp below, marking the steepest inversion in six weeks as dealers prepare for vol reset. Broad equity IV ranks have spiked to 90th percentile across SPY, EEM, and IWM while put skew reaches extreme 99th percentile levels. Credit spreads remain contained despite equity vol expansion, creating a cross-asset divergence that typically resolves within 48 hours.

VRP: Front-month IV trading 4-6 points above realized vol across major indices — aggressive vol selling premium available for patient capital
SKEW: Put skew at 99th percentile in SPY/QQQ signals maximum defensive positioning and creates asymmetric call upside reward
TERM STRUCTURE: Steep backwardation in high-beta names (AMD, META, ARM) with front-week premiums 15-20% rich to fair value

Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 12:31 ET
Negative gamma amplifies ARM CPU catalyst while dispersion trades emerge
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. ARM's CPU market entry drives explosive two-way flow (+104% calls, +156% puts) into $-15.7B dealer negative gamma that amplifies all moves.
  • 2. Index puts (1.37 P/C) diverge from single-name calls (0.97 P/C) as low 0.27 implied correlation signals dispersion opportunity.
  • 3. Core PCE tomorrow and NFP next week create time pressure as institutions position defensively through XLF blocks.
NARRATIVE — negative gamma amplifies

ARM's entry into the CPU market against Intel triggers the most violent options flow of the session, with calls surging +104% and puts exploding +156% as the stock trades at technical extremes. The two-way flow reflects genuine uncertainty about ARM's ability to penetrate Intel's datacenter dominance, with the unusual put-call parity suggesting sophisticated positioning around the +14.8% gap higher. This volatility explosion occurs within a $-15.7B SPY negative gamma environment where dealer hedging amplifies every directional move — ARM's technical score of 84.3 despite a death cross underscores the fundamental catalyst overwhelming chart patterns. The broader derivatives market reveals institutional dispersion positioning as index P/C ratios hit 1.37 while single-name ratios remain balanced at 0.97 — a 0.4x divergence indicating hedge fund index protection paired with single-name opportunity hunting. Low 0.27 implied correlation confirms this stock-specific regime, with 11 names in backwardation suggesting near-term event risk across individual securities. The 66,000-contract XLF put block spanning September through December reflects financial sector hedging ahead of tomorrow's Core PCE and next week's NFP — critical data points that could reshape the Federal Reserve's rate trajectory. Cross-asset positioning shows energy leading with XLE's 89.7 technical score and overbought RSI, while mega-cap technology faces headwinds as MSFT hits oversold levels and GOOG attracts defensive put flow despite institutional conviction from Tepper and Ackman.

Layer 1: Sentiment -15Layer 2: GEX -$15.7BLayer 3: P/C divergence 1.37/0.97Layer 8: ARM CPU newsLayer 9: Core PCE 1d

GREEK PRESSURE KEY
+$M = dealer buys to re-hedge
−$M = dealer sells to re-hedge
Charm: time-decay drift only. Vanna: vol-shift only. Not blended.
